nodes.label: PyTorch
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Chosen as the deep learning framework for the traffic management research project due to its support for dynamic computational graphs, debugging transparency, and strong research ecosystem, which align with the project's requirements for adaptive model evolution and custom traffic optimization targets.

nodes.label: Dynamic Computational Graph Architecture
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Employs an imperative, define-by-run approach where graphs are built dynamically during execution. This supports: 1) Adaptive Model Evolution: modifying network structures between training iterations without restarts to handle complex temporal traffic patterns; 2) Conditional Logic Integration: incorporating Python control flow for rush hour vs off-peak scenarios; 3) Iterative Research Cycle: accelerating hypothesis testing by eliminating compile-then-execute overhead.

nodes.label: Debugging and Development Transparency
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Prioritizes developer intuition and transparency through: 1) Native Python Integration: tensors behave like NumPy arrays to reduce context-switching and integration complexity; 2) Eager execution reporting runtime errors immediately with Python stack traces; 3) Interactive inspection of values, operations, and hypotheses in real-time.

nodes.label: Research and Experimentation Ecosystem
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Provides alignment with the academic machine learning community, giving direct access to cutting-edge research in time-series forecasting, reinforcement learning, and computer vision. Features modular components (torch.nn) and flexible automatic differentiation (autograd).

nodes.label: torch.nn Module
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: A comprehensive and flexible collection of neural network components that can be easily extended or customized for traffic modeling requirements.

nodes.label: PyTorch Autograd
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: An automatic differentiation system that gives fine-grained control over gradient computation, essential for custom loss functions capturing traffic management optimization targets (e.g. balancing wait time equity vs throughput).

nodes.label: Traffic Management Research
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: The core application domain of the project at the intersection of transportation engineering and machine learning. Involves modeling complex traffic patterns with temporal dependencies, rush-hour scenarios, and optimization goals like wait time equity and total throughput.
